narrative.headline: 2022 Honda Ridgeline: exterior lighting leads the complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 110 complaints with NHTSA. Exterior lighting is the most reported area at 27%, followed by the engine (21%) and electrical system (17%). No deaths and no injuries appear in the top areas except forward collision avoidance, which has 1 injury. A handful of reports mention fire (1 engine, 2 electrical, 1 power train), so those areas are worth a close look.

issues.title: Exterior Lighting
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 30 reports (27%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Owners describe the automatic high beams behaving erratically. Test the lights and high-beam automation on a drive.

issues.sample: 2022 HONDA RIDGELINE. CONSUMER WRITES IN REGARD TO DRIVER SIDE WINDOW MALFUNCTION.  THE CONSUMER STATED WHILE DRIVING THE VEHICLE LOST POWER. THE MALFUNCTION INDICATOR LIGHT ILLUMINATED. THERE WAS NO ENGINE RPM RESPONSE TO THE THROTTLE PEDAL APPLICATION. THE ENGINE WOULD INCREASE
issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 23 reports (21%), tied to 1 crash and 1 fire. Watch for loss of power or warning lights while driving.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 19 reports (17%), including 2 with fire. The fire count makes this worth checking carefully even though no crashes or injuries are reported.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: 13 reports (12%), linked to 1 crash and 1 injury. Owners describe unexpected brake warnings or activation. The only top area with a reported injury.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: The smallest top area: 11 reports (10%), with 1 fire and no crashes or injuries. Some owners report trouble shifting into reverse.
